events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need assess scenario: unprotected trench ≥5 ft with workers in it, actively in progress. OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires protective systems for trenches 5 ft or deeper unless excavation is in stable rock. Workers in unprotected trench is high/critical hazard, cave-in. Decision fail. Risk critical. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions: non-compliant, installed-defective? Trench protection absent, so non-compliant. Findings: workers in trench 5+ ft without protective system, no sloping/shoring/shielding, potential cave-in. Actions: evacuate workers, install protective system (sloping, benching, shoring, trench box), competent person inspect, barricade, permit. References: OSHA 1926 Subpart P, 1926.650-652, 1926.652 requirements for protective systems. Need output exactly JSON.

    "Workers are inside a trench 5 feet or deeper with no protective system in place.",
    "Exposure to cave-in hazard; soil classification and protective system are unknown/unverified.",
    "Condition violates OSHA excavation requirements for trenches 5 ft or deeper."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately evacuate all workers from the trench.",
    "Prohibit entry until an adequate protective system is installed (sloping, benching, shoring, or trench shield).",
    "Have a competent person inspect the excavation and classify the soil before re-entry.",
    "Install barricades, warning lines, and access controls around the excavation.",
    "Verify utilities, egress spacing, and atmospheric hazards are addressed per the excavation plan."
  ],
  "rationale": "OSHA 1926 Subpart P requires a protective system for any trench 5 feet or deeper unless the excavation is entirely in stable rock. Workers in an unprotected trench at or above this depth are at imminent risk of cave-in, so the work fails inspection and must stop until protection is provided.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart P - Excavations",
    "29 CFR 1926.650 - Scope, application, and definitions",
    "29 CFR 1926.651 - Specific excavation requirements",
    "29 CFR 1926.652 - Requirements for protective systems"
  ]
}
